notes: Plaintiff opening work notes, 2026-06-29T18:41Z. Opportunity openings:plaintiff; final action allowed record_opening_statement only, no evidence submission. Checked current record via get_case/list_evidence/stat/read. Three visible case-packet files, no prior arguments/exhibits. ev_74d969c640f7_market-question states market title, strike Ronaldo, challenged No/requested Yes, and core dispute: whether market phrase "during the FIFA World Cup Draw" includes Trump's public remarks at the venue immediately before the formal draw program began, or only the formal on-stage program. ev_7de1d3f2c404_formal-draw-program states draft complaint says the formal draw-program transcript does not appear to contain "Ronaldo"; it is only a starting point and not the full formal transcript. ev_af2671376951_pre-event-remarks states draft complaint attributes to Trump's public remarks at the venue immediately before the formal program: "I got to meet Ronaldo the other day." and "Ronaldo is great." It is also only a starting point, not a full upstream transcript. Source map: decisive elements are market wording/rules, scope of "during," exact pre-event words, timing/venue/context, and whether formal program was exclusive. Strong adverse theory: market meant only formal on-stage program/transcript; packet admits that source apparently lacks Ronaldo. Native search before opening: searched exact market phrase and quoted remarks. A public lead (Bernard Bulletin/Substack dispute bible snippet) appears to discuss same strike and red-carpet/pre-program livestream, but it is not admitted and should not be cited as record proof in opening; preserve as later source target. Current theory: opening should frame dispute as event-scope interpretation; admitted packet supports that the remarks were public, at venue, immediately before formal program, and alleged to include Ronaldo, while also acknowledging missing upstream transcript/video and governing market rules as later proof targets. Durable notes saved at /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md.

Defendant opening opportunity openings:defendant. Waited as instructed; initial state waiting on plaintiff opening, re-polled until ready. Inspected get_case and list_evidence. Visible record has only three system case-packet files plus plaintiff opening; no submitted evidence/exhibits. Stat/read exact bytes of: ev_74d969c640f7_market-question (market title, strike Ronaldo, challenged No, requested Yes, core dispute whether pre-formal venue remarks are within 'during' or only formal on-stage program); ev_7de1d3f2c404_formal-draw-program (draft complaint says formal draw-program transcript does not appear to contain Ronaldo; file is only a starting point and not full formal transcript); ev_af2671376951_pre-event-remarks (draft complaint attributes two Ronaldo lines to Trump public remarks at venue immediately before formal program; file is starting point and not full upstream transcript). Plaintiff opening argues ordinary event-language scope includes immediate venue remarks and acknowledges missing primary source chain.

Source map and native investigation before opening: decisive elements are market rules/resolution criteria, official program boundary/start time, full formal program transcript/video/captions, full pre-event remarks source/video/captions, and whether pre-event/red-carpet/press feed was official covered event or separate pre-show. Web searches performed: exact market title + Ronaldo; exact quoted packet lines; Trump/Ronaldo/FIFA World Cup Draw; site:kalshi.com title and related terms; date/title phrase. Leads found but not record evidence: Kalshi tag snippets mention the market but did not expose rules; Bernard Bulletin secondary 'Kalshi Mention Market Dispute Bible' describes this exact item as red-carpet press remarks prior to programme, rules Version/Timeframe, result N, useful adverse/search lead for defense but not admitted; social/video snippets suggest pre-event/repost material with weak provenance. Journal updated at /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md.

Evidence analysis: current packet proves the dispute exists and frames timing as immediately before formal program, but does not prove governing rules, official timing, source version, or complete transcripts. Formal-program packet supports defense but is not complete primary proof. Pre-event packet supports plaintiff on utterance if credited but has weak provenance/custody because it is attributed to draft complaint and lacks upstream transcript. Defense opening should not deny possible utterance; focus on scope and burden: proposition requires counting pre-program remarks as during the draw, and current record leaves that unproven. Later phases should seek Kalshi rules, official draw program source, formal transcript, red-carpet/source video, timestamps, and archive/source-chain evidence.

Plaintiff merits work notes, 2026-06-29T18:46Z. Opportunity arguments:plaintiff; allowed submit_evidence and submit_argument. Rechecked record/evidence with get_case/list_evidence/stat. Record now includes both openings, still only three case-packet evidence items before plaintiff submissions. Defendant opening argues 'during' means formal on-stage program only and stresses missing primary source. Source investigation: rendered Kalshi page from Substack link https://kalshi.com/markets/kxtrumpmentionb/trump-mention-b/kxtrumpmentionb-25dec06 exposed shared market title and rules; relevant rule: video of FIFA World Cup Draw primarily used to resolve market, transcripts secondary if no consensus, event must be opened to press for live televised/streamed event. Limitation: rendered page displayed Canada strike KXTRUMPMENTIONB-25DEC06-CANA, not exact Ronaldo strike, but case packet supplies Ronaldo strike and page is same Trump World Cup Draw event/series. Direct curl to Kalshi page returned Vercel Security Checkpoint. Guessed Kalshi API ticker https://api.elections.kalshi.com/trade-api/v2/markets/KXTRUMPMENTIONB-25DEC06-RONALDO returned not_found; not used as proof. Rendered Bernard Bulletin/Substack dispute index at https://bernardbulletin.substack.com/p/kalshi-mention-market-dispute-bible includes exact entry: 12-5-25, What will Trump say during the FIFA World Cup Draw, strike Ronaldo, evidence Trump said strike to press on red carpet prior to programme, livestreamed, rules Version/Timeframe, result N, last trade Y $.10. Limitation: secondary source, no warranty, adverse reported result N; use for corroborating exact dispute/source-chain and showing issue is timeframe/version, not absence of word. Submitted evidence accepted: ev_026c90725176_submitted-evidence-01-plaintiff-026c90725176 (Kalshi extracted rules), ev_4bed0ef76dcf_submitted-evidence-02-plaintiff-4bed0ef76dcf (public dispute index entry). Argument plan: offer three packet files plus two submitted items. Concede missing primary video/full transcript but argue preponderance favors Yes: packet directly attributes two Ronaldo lines in public venue remarks immediately before program; Kalshi rules prioritize video over transcript and tie market to press/live streamed event; secondary index corroborates red-carpet/livestream source and exact Ronaldo dispute even though reported result was N. Durable notes updated at /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md.

Defendant arguments opportunity. At start, reviewed get_case/list_evidence and exact reads of plaintiff's new evidence. Plaintiff ev_026c907... is a rendered Kalshi page extraction, but exact text shows it selected/displayed Canada strike, including 'Resolves Yes if Donald Trump says Canada...' and market ticker KXTRUMPMENTIONB-25DEC06-CANA; it is not Ronaldo-specific, though it includes event title/rule language. Plaintiff ev_4bed0... is Bernard Bulletin secondary dispute index; exact text says Ronaldo entry, red carpet prior to start of programme, livestreamed, rules Version/Timeframe, Result N, Last trade Yes $.10, with no-warranty/no-precedent disclaimer. Native investigation: searched ticker variants and exact event IDs; guessed API markets KXTRUMPMENTIONB-25DEC06-RONALDO and ...-RONA returned not_found; Kalshi public API event endpoint KXTRUMPMENTIONB-25DEC06 returned parent event metadata (title, event ticker, date subtitle, settlement sources, markets empty); Coinbase event URL direct curl returned Cloudflare challenge. Submitted defendant evidence ev_bc775d9... with the Kalshi public API event response and retrieval notes. Updated /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md. Planned argument: plaintiff has not met burden because no primary Ronaldo-specific market rule/page, no primary video/transcript/captions, no official timing/program boundary, no Kalshi determination notice. PX-4 is Canada-specific and cannot prove Ronaldo-specific terms or that red-carpet press before programme was 'as part of' the draw. PX-5 is secondary and if credited favors defense on decisive issue because it reports Result N and labels dispute Version/Timeframe, confirming the problem is not whether a word was said somewhere but whether it was said in the covered version/timeframe.

Plaintiff rebuttal work notes, 2026-06-29T19:03Z. Opportunity rebuttals:plaintiff. Rechecked record and evidence. Defendant added ev_bc775d9fe77b_submitted-evidence-03-defendant-bc775d9fe77b, Kalshi public API parent-event JSON. Exact read: retrieval notes say guessed Ronaldo market endpoints KXTRUMPMENTIONB-25DEC06-RONALDO and -RONA returned not_found and are not asserted market facts; event_response.event confirms event_ticker KXTRUMPMENTIONB-25DEC06, series KXTRUMPMENTIONB, category Mentions, sub_title On Dec 5, 2025, title What will Trump say during the FIFA World Cup Draw?, and settlement_sources list including NYT/AP/Reuters/etc and White House; markets array is empty. Rebuttal target: defense uses missing Ronaldo-specific primary source and PX-5 reported result N to argue No. Response: defendant API exhibit is silent on formal-only boundary and corroborates parent event; absence of markets in that endpoint does not negate case-packet Ronaldo strike or event-level rules. PX-5 is secondary and not binding, but if considered it confirms the strike was said to press on red carpet and livestreamed; reported No shows dispute, not correctness. Concede limitation of Canada page but argue case packet supplies Ronaldo strike and defendant API supplies same parent event; no record evidence shows a different Ronaldo-specific exclusion. No new evidence needed. Durable notes updated at /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md.

Surrebuttal opportunity. Rechecked get_case/list_evidence. No new plaintiff source evidence was submitted in rebuttal; plaintiff added a technical report and re-offered defendant API evidence as PX-R1. Rebuttal argues the API exhibit is silent on formal-program-only exclusion, Canada-page limitation was disclosed, PX-5 confirms utterance/red-carpet/livestream facts if given weight, and absence of an admitted exclusion means Yes. Analysis: surrebuttal should be narrow and answer the burden point. Defense does not need to prove a formal-only exclusion; plaintiff must prove inclusion of pre-program red-carpet/press remarks within the market-covered draw. The admitted record repeatedly says immediately before formal program / prior to start of programme. PX-4 is Canada-specific and 'as part of FIFA World Cup Draw' is the unresolved predicate, not proof that red carpet counts. PX-5 cannot be used as reliable for utterance while disregarding its same-entry Result N and Version/Timeframe characterization. No additional source evidence found/needed for surrebuttal; journal updated.

Plaintiff closing work notes, 2026-06-29T19:06Z. Opportunity closings:plaintiff. Closing only; no new evidence or reports allowed. Rechecked get_case/list_evidence and stat for submitted evidence. Record now includes defendant surrebuttal. Defendant surrebuttal argues plaintiff bears burden, silence on exclusion is not proof of inclusion, timing labels say immediately before formal program / red carpet prior to programme / Version-Timeframe / Result N, and PX-5 is too weak if not accepted for result. Closing plan: candidly admit weaknesses (no full primary video/transcript; PX-4 displayed Canada strike on shared event page; PX-5 secondary/adverse). Emphasize preponderance, not certainty. Plaintiff has affirmative record support: packet identifies Ronaldo strike and exact scope dispute; pre-event packet attributes two Ronaldo lines to Trump's public remarks at venue immediately before formal program; formal-program packet only says formal transcript appears not to contain Ronaldo and is not full transcript; Kalshi page extraction says video primary and transcripts secondary, with press/live streamed event contemplated; dispute index corroborates red carpet/livestream for exact Ronaldo strike while its reported N is not binding; defendant API confirms parent event and sources but markets [] and no exclusion. Closing ask: ordinary event/livestream scope covers public venue red-carpet remarks immediately before formal program more likely than a formal transcript-only theory. Durable notes updated at /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md.

Closing opportunity. Final get_case/list_evidence scan done. Evidence unchanged: three case-packet files, plaintiff Canada-specific Kalshi extraction, plaintiff Bernard Bulletin secondary index, defendant Kalshi API parent-event response. Plaintiff closing admits record is imperfect and timing is strongest defense point, but argues live public venue coverage immediately before formal program should count absent exclusion. Closing theory: plaintiff must prove both utterance and that the utterance is in covered event scope. Record may plausibly show a Ronaldo utterance near the event, but timing/scope evidence favors defense: market packet says immediately before formal draw program; secondary index says red carpet prior to start of programme, labels dispute Version/Timeframe, reports Result N. Canada page cannot carry Ronaldo-specific scope; 'video primary' does not mean every public feed segment counts, and its own rule text says Canada/as part of FIFA World Cup Draw. Defendant API confirms parent event but no Ronaldo-specific market/rule/video/transcript/determination. Missing primary source chain remains decisive under plaintiff's burden. Journal updated.
